shared_operational_field: both lanes populate {shadow, body, numeric lossiness measure, control, restoring structure} from committed receipts; both sit on the determine/resist axis (lossy shadow + named structure that restores the body)

typed_mismatch.detail: Kakeya lossiness = finite body-count per signature; Hodge lossiness = decoder overclaim rate (existence-undecidability). Same frame and same decoder-non-invertibility measurement, different units (count vs rate).
measure_types: finite count of bodies per shadow signature
decoder overclaim rate (shadow does not determine cycle existence)

falsifier.name: COLLISION_TABLE_ONLY_RHYME
falsifier.reason: Both rows populate the same operational schema from committed receipts (numeric, control-backed lossiness + restoring structure), so the table guides probes, not just rhymes. Caveat: the lossiness measure is typed differently per lane (count vs rate).

rows.shadow: direction-coverage signature (which directions contain a full line)

lossiness_measure.type: finite count of bodies per shadow signature

control.name: random same-size body baseline (H-K4)
control.caveat: count is a finite-grid density artifact unless measured as excess over a size-matched control

rows.restoring_structure: Dvir polynomial method - complete shadow forces size >= q(q+1)/2 (dvirFloor q5=15, q7=28, q11=66)
rows.determine_resist: finite resist: size determined (Dvir floor), exact body not

lossiness_measure.type: decoder overclaim rate (shadow does not determine cycle existence)

control.name: two independent semantic judges + lexical-vs-judge agreement (H-K1 PHASE4F)
control.caveat: lexical route check over-credits: judge-consensus vs lexical agreement 0.6; inter-judge 0.8

rows.restoring_structure: Lefschetz (1,1) / hard Lefschetz / Cattani-Deligne-Kaplan in special cases; general case open (the Hodge conjecture)
rows.determine_resist: infinite resist in general: existence undecidable by the shadow (sigma = infinity)
